page: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is a high-capacity general-purpose server belonging to the Dadsv7 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture, delivering 160 dedicated vCPUs across 80 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core. The system is configured with 640.0 GB of RAM, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of memory per core. Local storage is bundled with a capacity of 9449 GB, and virtualization is managed via Microsoft Hyper-V.

Benchmark results place this server in the top tier for compute and memory performance. The AMD EPYC processor achieves top-tier scores in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests, alongside a PassMark CPU score of 145,385.56. Memory performance is characterized by a small-block cached read bandwidth of 14,521.33 GB/sec and a large-block write bandwidth of 210.38 GB/sec. Hardware-level acceleration is present for cryptographic operations via AES and VAES, as well as vector calculations through AVX-512 flags.

This hardware profile is highly optimized for resource-intensive workloads that require substantial local storage and high memory throughput without dedicated GPU acceleration. It demonstrates exceptional efficiency for database operations, executing 204.26 million Redis SET operations per second and achieving top-tier PassMark database scores. It is also well-suited for high-throughput static web serving and CPU-based large language model inference, processing 73.9 tokens per second on medium 7B models.
description: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is a general-purpose server from the Dadsv7 family, featuring an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs, 80 physical cores, and 640.0 GB of RAM. It includes 9449 GB of local storage and runs on Microsoft Hyper-V. The server delivers top-tier performance across CPU, memory, and database benchmarks, achieving 287,722.37 on stress-ng multi-core and 204.26 million operations per second on Redis SET. With hardware-accelerated cryptography and vector instructions, it is highly optimized for large-scale databases, high-throughput web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is a Dadsv7-family server with 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage, delivering top-tier database and CPU-based LLM inference performance.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D160ads_v7 is a Dadsv7 server featuring 160 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 640 GB RAM, and 9449 GB local storage for high-performance database workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity general-purpose server featuring 160 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, massive local storage, and top-tier database and memory performance.
bullet_points: Powered by AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 160 dedicated vCPUs and 80 physical cores
Equipped with 640.0 GB of RAM and 9449 GB of local storage
Delivers top-tier memory bandwidth and multi-core CPU performance
Optimized for high-throughput database operations and Redis workloads
Supports CPU-based LLM inference with strong token generation speeds
